data.title: Nucor Non-GAAP EPS of $0.77, revenue of $9.5B beats by $640M
data.description:
• Nucor press release (NUE): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.77.
  • Revenue of $9.5B (+21.3% Y/Y) beats by $640M.
  • Consolidated net earnings attributable to Nucor stockholders of $743 million, or $3.23 per diluted share, for the first quarter of 2026.

  • On February 20, 2026, Nucor's Board of Directors declared a cash dividend of $0.56 per share. This cash dividend is payable on May 11, 2026, to stockholders of record as of March 31, 2026 and is Nucor's 212th consecutive quarterly cash dividend.

data.title: Gulf Resources receives Nasdaq notice over delayed annual filing
data.description:
• Gulf Resources (GURE) on Monday said it has received a delinquency notice from Nasdaq after failing to timely file its annual report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025.
  • The company said the notice does not immediately affect its Nasdaq listing.
  • Gulf Resources said it has 60 days to submit a plan to regain compliance and could be granted up to 180 days, until October 12, 2026, to file the report if its plan is accepted. If not, it may appeal the decision.
  • The company said it is working to complete the filing.
  • GURE +0.24% after hours to $3.5386.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stocks to watch on Monday after hours: CDNS, BBBY, AMKR
data.description:
Wall Street ended mixed on Monday as investors braced for a pivotal week—eyeing Big Tech earnings, the upcoming Federal Reserve decision, and ongoing developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) ended +0.1%, while the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) closed +0.2%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) finished -0.1%.

Now, here are the 3 stocks to watch on Monday after hours:

  • Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) shares rose 2.8% in late trading on Monday after the electronic design automation firm posted first-quarter results that were above Wall Street's forecast.
  • Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY) recorded its first quarter of significant revenue growth in almost five years, with an increase of 9.4% year after year after adjusting for the retailer's exit from Canada. During the quarter, the company saw better engagement, stronger conversion, and improvements in average order value. Shares jumped 20% in extended trading.
  • Amkor Technology (AMKR) shares declined 1.5% even after the semiconductor packaging and test services company reported earnings per share of $0.33 versus the consensus estimate of $0.24.

data.title: FB Financial authorizes $175M share repurchase program
data.description:
• FB Financial (FBK) on Monday said its board of directors has approved a new authorization to repurchase up to $175 million of its common stock.
  • The program will remain in effect until June 30, 2027, replacing a prior authorization set to expire in January 2027.
  • The company said repurchases may be made through open market purchases or private transactions, depending on market conditions and other factors including regulatory requirements and capital needs.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: agilon health appoints new CEO
data.description:
• agilon health (AGL) named Tim O’Rourke as chief executive officer and board member, effective May 7.
  • O’Rourke will succeed Ronald A. Williams, who will remain chairman of the board.

data.title: Ranger Energy Services GAAP EPS of $0.12 misses by $0.11, revenue of $159.1M beats by $2.63M
data.description:
• Ranger Energy Services press release (RNGR): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.12 misses by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $159.1M (+17.7% Y/Y) beats by $2.63M.

data.title: Universal Health Services Non-GAAP EPS of $5.62 beats by $0.16, revenue of $4.49B beats by $100M
data.description:
• Universal Health Services press release (UHS): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $5.62 beats by $0.16.

data.title: The Joint to sell 45 clinics for $2.3M; shifts to franchise-only model
data.description:
• The Joint (JYNT) announced on Monday that it will sell 45 company-run clinics in Southern California to Elite Chiro Group for about $2.3M.
  • Out of these, Elite Chiro Group has already taken over daily operations of 32 clinics from April 27, 2026, under a temporary management deal until full ownership transfer.
  • The remaining 13 clinics will also be fully handed over once lease and ownership formalities are completed.
  • After this deal and two other pending sales, The Joint (JYNT) will be left with just 3 company-managed clinics out of a total of 960 locations, moving closer to a franchise-only model.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nvidia chief accounting officer to retire, set to be replaced by former Intel exec
data.description:
• Nvidia (NVDA) disclosed on Monday that Donald Robertson, vice president and chief accounting officer, is set to retire from the company, effective May 4.
  • Robertson will remain with Nvidia as VP, Finance to support various projects until July 1, the company added. He will be replaced by former Intel (INTC) chief accounting officer Scott Gawel, who resigned from the company this past Friday.
  • Gawel will have a base salary of $800,000 and will be granted two new hire equity grants of restricted stock units with a combined target value of $12.875M. The RSUs will vest approximately over a four-year period according to Nvidia's standard vesting schedules. 
  • Nvidia shares gained nearly 4% on Monday, while Intel shares added 2%.

data.title: Amkor declines after Q1 results feature 27% revenue jump
data.description:
Amkor Technology (AMKR) shares declined during early post-market trading on Monday after it released its first quarter 2026 financial results.

For the quarter ended March 31, the semiconductor packaging and test services company reported earnings per share of $0.33 versus the consensus estimate of $0.24.

Revenue for the quarter increased 27% year over year to $1.68B, which was more than the $1.65B estimate.

Looking ahead, Amkor expects its second-quarter sales to range from $1.75B to $1.85B, which clears the $1.74B estimate. It expects net income ranging from $105M to $130M and EPS of $0.42 to $0.52. It provided guidance of 14.5% to 15.5% for its gross margin.

Furthermore, it projects its 2026 capital expenditures to range from $2.5B to $3B.

The company paid a quarterly dividend of $0.08352 per share on March 31. Last week, the Board of Directors authorized the repurchase of up to $300M of the company's common stock.

"Amkor delivered a strong start to 2026 with record first-quarter revenue driven by broad-based end market demand," said CEO Kevin Engel. "During the quarter, we progressed key customer programs in advanced packaging, improved utilization across our factory network, and made continued progress on our margin initiatives, demonstrating our ability to execute effectively in a dynamic industry environment."
